summary: Crisp soundboard capture of a solid ’73 Dead show with standout Uncle John’s Band/Playing‑in‑the‑Band sandwich and a beautiful Brokedown Palace.
review: The Charlie Miller transfer delivers clear, well‑balanced audio that lets the vocals and instruments shine, though a few listeners note occasional graininess and low volume. Musically the concert is enjoyable – fans rave about the Uncle John’s Band sandwich, the Playing‑in‑the‑Band sequence, Me & My Uncle banter and a mellow Brokedown Palace – even if some feel the overall pacing is a bit sluggish compared to the surrounding dates.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise both the sound quality and key performances, with only a handful of criticisms about pacing or volume; the weighted average lands in the good‑to‑great range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Consistently described as crisp, clear and top‑notch; a few comments mention slight graininess, low overall volume, but no major dropouts or balance issues.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is noted for lively second‑set banter (Bobby & Phil clearing aisles, a comedic rattlesnake vamp) and a memorable Playing‑in‑the‑Band > Uncle John’s Band > Playing‑in‑the‑Band sandwich; overall pacing is described as solid but a bit sluggish by some listeners.

band_member_comments.Phil: Calmer approach after Me and My Uncle, helped sway disobedient heads back to their seats.
band_member_comments.Bob: Screamed twice at the audience during Me and My Uncle, an unusually aggressive moment for the drummer.
band_member_comments.Drums: Same as Bob – Bobby (Bill Kreutzmann) was notably forceful on stage, shouting "Get back!" during Me and My Uncle.
